scaled_oracle_cases.description: Test deblur_system_call with special characters in file path, duplicate params, and param values with spaces, ensuring robustness and handling of richer input variants.
scaled_oracle_cases.semantic_intent: The solution must handle a file path with spaces, duplicate parameter flags, and parameter values containing spaces, without hardcoding assumptions or failing.
scaled_oracle_cases.target_constraint: Support file path with spaces, duplicate flags (e.g., '--min-size' appearing twice), and param values with spaces.
scaled_oracle_cases.setup_code:
logging.basicConfig(level=logging.DEBUG)
import tempfile
import os
# Create a FASTA file with spaces in the name to test file_path handling
temp_dir = tempfile.mkdtemp()
input_fp = os.path.join(temp_dir, 'my sequences.fasta')
with open(input_fp, 'w') as f:
    f.write(">seq1\nACTG\n>seq2\nTGCA\n")
scaled_oracle_cases.call_code:
params = ['--min-size', '50', '--min-size', '100', '--log-level', 'DEBUG WITH SPACE']
stdout, stderr, returncode = deblur_system_call(params, input_fp)
result = {'stdout': stdout, 'stderr': stderr, 'returncode': returncode}
print('STDOUT:', stdout)
print('STDERR:', stderr)
print('Return code:', returncode)
covered_requirements: Scaled executable cases must expose the new input/data complexity introduced by this operator.
Scaled executable cases must expose the new anti-shortcut robustness requirement introduced by this operator.
Handle duplicate/conflicting inputs and randomized names correctly.
Do not hardcode common filenames, flags, or only-happy-path inputs.
Support richer file/data shapes such as missing sections, altered formatting, or content-based parsing.
